content: We can hypothesize that integrating a differentiable episodic-semantic node representation learning framework into the 2025 generation of ICM curiosity-driven autonomous agents will resolve the common 'novelty saturation' problem, where agents stop exploring after encountering all easily distinguishable new states. Episodic node representations will encode raw, contextual details of newly visited states to detect raw novelty, while semantic node representations will abstract generalizable structural patterns from those episodes to continuously generate implicit new sub-curiosities about unobserved structural variations even when all surface-level states have been explored.
